cohort_binding_note: Sub-gates (b) and (c) are absence predicates (AppRequests empty / AppTraces empty) that are interpretable as missing-env-var evidence ONLY in conjunction with sub-gate (a) (env=null on the :v3 'app' container) AND sub-gate (d) (the app served 20 HTTP 200 during the before-fix capture window). Without (a)+(d), 'AI tables empty' could mean SDK is broken / workspace is misrouted / query is wrong / no traffic was sent — none of which is attributable to the missing APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING env var. The predicate_inputs block colocates the anchor and the absences in the same gate JSON so a reader cannot interpret them separately.
sub_gate_a_predicate: 01-env-before-fix.env is None AND .image endswith ':v3' AND .containerName == 'app' (BOTH-not-OR across all three conditions). Single-path. Record-scoped: parsed JSON field reads.
sub_gate_b_predicate: 03-ai-requests-before-fix.tables[0].rows == [] (exact equality, single-path). Record-scoped: parsed JSON array equality, not greppy substring.
sub_gate_c_predicate: 04-ai-traces-before-fix.tables[0].rows == [] (exact equality, single-path). Record-scoped: parsed JSON array equality, not greppy substring.
sub_gate_d_predicate: 00-trigger-run.txt contains exactly 20 lines matching pattern stripped.startswith('request ') AND 'HTTP 200' in stripped. Line-scoped: split on newline, strip, prefix-and-substring check; NOT whole-file regex. This availability anchor makes (b)/(c) attributable to missing instrumentation rather than 'app down'.
